title: 10 best herbal essential oils for difficulty chewing
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for difficulty chewing are a natural solution to alleviate discomfort and improve oral function. These oils, extracted from plants like ginger, peppermint, and chamomile, help soothe sore muscles and joints in the jaw and mouth, making it easier to chew and swallow. By using a carrier oil and applying a few drops of herbal essential oil to the affected area, individuals with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders or fibromyalgia can experience reduced pain and inflammation, allowing for more comfortable eating and improved overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with difficulty chewing because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. It contains a compound called glycyrretinic acid, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth and throat, making it easier to chew and swallow. Additionally, licorice oil has natural antibacterial properties that can help to combat infections and promote healing in the gums and oral mucosa, further alleviating difficulty chewing. Overall, licorice essential oils provides a natural and effective solution for those experiencing difficulties with chewing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal licorice essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and pain in the jaw, mouth, and gums, making it easier to chew food.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of herbal licorice essential oil help protect against free radicals that can damage oral tissues, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anesthetic and analgesic: Herbal licorice essential oil's anesthetic and analgesic properties numb the pain and discomfort associated with difficulty chewing, making it easier to eat and maintain a balanced diet.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in alleviating gum inflammation and pain associated with difficulty chewing.
Barks: Licorice bark is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth and gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots weigh 500 grams and chop into small pieces.
Dry the chopped glycyrrhiza glabra root fragments in a warm oven at 60 degrees celsius for eight hours.
Steam distill the dried glycyrrhiza glabra root material using a clevenger apparatus with distilled water and collect essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and sediment.
Store the final herbal glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil in dark glass vials at room temperature for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with difficulty chewing because of its calming and relaxing properties that alleviate muscle tension in the jaw, face, and mouth. The oil's antispasmodic effects help to reduce involuntary contractions that can make it difficult to chew or swallow, while its soothing qualities ease discomfort and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, lemon balm essential oils provide a natural solution for individuals struggling with difficulty chewing.
remedy_properties: Soothing and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lemon balm essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw, mouth, and gums, making it easier to chew and swallow.

Antioxidant and Antimicrobial Properties: Lemon balm essential oil's antioxidant properties can protect against oral infections and inflammation, while its antimicrobial properties can help combat bacteria and viruses that may be contributing to difficulty chewing.

Calming and Sedative Effects: The calming properties of lemon balm essential oil can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common factors in tension headaches and jaw pain that can make eating and chewing difficult.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and discomfort associated with difficulty chewing.
Flowers: The flowers of the lemon balm plant contain flavonoids and terpenes that help to relax muscle spasms and ease jaw tension, alleviating difficulties in chewing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves from a pesticide-free area or purchase dried herbs.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 24-48 hours.
Steam distill the dried melissa officinalis leaves using a still or a professional essential oil distillation kit.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter paper into dark glass bottles within 30 minutes.
Store the finished melissa officinalis essential oil in a cool, dark place for at least 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with difficulty chewing because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, peppermint oil can help ease tension in the jaw muscles and reduce pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. Additionally, peppermint's cooling sensation can provide a soothing relief for sore teeth and gums, making it easier to chew and swallow food. This natural remedy can be especially beneficial for individuals experiencing chronic mouth and facial pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and pain associated with conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder, which can cause difficulty chewing.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of peppermint essential oil can help alleviate toothache pain and gum inflammation, making it easier to chew food without discomfort.
Antispasmodic: Peppermint essential oil's antispasmodic properties can relax muscle spasms in the jaw and face, reducing tension and cramping that can make chewing difficult.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots have a rich concentration of menthol, which helps to ease pain and discomfort associated with difficulty chewing.
Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant contain high levels of cineole, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or local market in the morning.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves in a warm place away from direct sunlight for 24 hours.
Steam distill 100 grams of dried peppermint leaves with 500 ml of distilled water at 100 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Collect the essential oil that separates from the distillation mixture and decant it into a dark glass bottle immediately after collection.
Store the mentha x piperita essential oil in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon essential oils helps with difficulty chewing because of its unique properties. The oil has a warming effect on the body, which can help increase saliva production and reduce inflammation in the mouth and jaw. This can make it easier to chew and digest food. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties can help soothe sore gums, jaw pain, and other issues that may be contributing to difficulty chewing.
remedy_properties: Analgesic and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Herbal Ceylon cinnamon essential oil's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce pain and swelling in the mouth and jaw, making it easier to chew and eat.

Antimicrobial Properties: The antimicrobial properties of Ceylon cinnamon essential oil can help combat oral infections and inflammation, which can contribute to difficulty chewing by reducing pain and discomfort.

Sedative and Relaxant Properties: Ceylon cinnamon essential oil's sedative and relaxant properties can help calm muscle spasms in the jaw and mouth, making it easier to chew and swallow, especially for individuals with conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Ceylon cinnamon contain sesquiterpenes, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving pain and discomfort associated with difficulty chewing.
Barks: The barks of Ceylon cinnamon contain cinnamic acid, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing, helping to alleviate symptoms of difficulty chewing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinnamomum verum leaves and stems from well-maintained plants for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade drying or low-temperature drying methods to preserve aroma.
Use a high-quality oil extraction device to distill the dried plant material into essential oil, yielding 30ml per batch.
Mix the extracted essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a 10% dilution ratio for topical application.
Store the prepared essential oil blend in dark glass containers at room temperature for up to six months while maintaining its therapeutic properties.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower essential oils helps with difficulty chewing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated jaw muscles and gums. The oil's analgesic properties also provide relief from pain and discomfort associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders. Additionally, Kansas coneflower's antimicrobial properties help combat infections that can cause gum inflammation and toothaches, making it easier to eat and chew without discomfort or pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Echinacea contains compounds that can reduce inflammation, which may help alleviate gum pain or swelling associated with difficulty chewing.
Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants present in Echinacea can help protect the mouth and gums from oxidative stress, which can contribute to oral health issues like gum disease or tooth decay.
Immunomodulatory properties: Echinacea has been shown to stimulate the immune system, which may help the body respond more effectively to infections that could be contributing to difficulty chewing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help soothe gum inflammation and reduce pain associated with difficulty chewing.
Seeds: The seeds contain bioactive compounds that aid in reducing swelling and alleviating discomfort in the mouth and jaw, making them ideal for difficulty chewing.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried echinacea angustifolia flowers and leaves in the amount of 100 grams per 500 milliliters of carrier oil.
Combine the dry botanical material with sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and place it in a warm dark location for 6 to 8 weeks to allow for steeping.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container after the steeping process is complete.
Allow the extracted oil to mature at room temperature for an additional 2-3 days before using it topically to ease difficulty chewing issues.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop essential oils helps with difficulty chewing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the mouth, throat, and jaw. Its expectorant qualities also help to loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier to swallow and chew food. Additionally, hyssop's analgesic properties provide relief from pain and discomfort associated with dental issues or oral surgery, allowing for smoother and more comfortable chewing. Overall, hyssop essential oils can be a natural and effective remedy for alleviating difficulty chewing caused by various oral health concerns.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Hyssop essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, jaw, and throat, making it easier to chew and swallow.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in hyssop essential oil can help combat bacterial infections and reduce gum inflammation, which can contribute to difficulty chewing due to swollen or sore gums.

Antioxidant properties: Hyssop essential oil's antioxidant properties can help protect the mouth and throat from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy tissue and reducing discomfort during chewing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hyssop roots are rich in compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing, making them effective in alleviating discomfort associated with difficulty chewing.
Buds: Hyssop buds contain flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can aid in soothing oral mucosa and promoting healthy mouth tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality hyssopus officinalis leaves at dawn when the oil content is highest within three to four weeks.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm and shaded area for seven to ten days to reduce moisture levels significantly.
Steam distill 500 grams of dried hyssopus officinalis leaves with 1 liter of distilled water for one to two hours producing an essential oil yield of 0.5%.
Filter the resulting essential oil using activated carbon and a coffee filter paper to remove impurities and sediment thoroughly within twenty minutes.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles sealed tightly and refrigerated at a temperature between 2°c to 8°c for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with difficulty chewing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth and jaw. The warming and soothing properties of ginger also help to relax tense muscles, making it easier to open the jaw and chew food effectively. Additionally, ginger has natural antibacterial properties that can combat bad breath and gum inflammation, further contributing to improved oral function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, can reduce swelling and pain associated with dental issues or TMJ disorders, making it easier to chew.

Antioxidant: Ginger's antioxidant properties can help protect the mouth and gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to difficulty chewing and discomfort while eating.

Digestive aid: Ginger's natural digestive enzymes and anti-inflammatory compounds can help ease digestion and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, nausea, or bloating that may be exacerbating difficulty chewing.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in volatile compounds, rhyzomes are the primary source of ginger's medicinal properties, making them ideal for extracting essential oils that can help alleviate difficulty chewing.

Roots: With their high concentration of active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, roots are a popular choice for creating essential oils that can provide relief from pain and discomfort associated with difficulty chewing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger rhizomes of zingiber officinale for optimal potency.
Chop the harvested ginger into small pieces weighing approximately 250 grams.
Steam distill the chopped ginger in a steam distillation apparatus for two hours.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil using a glass funnel and cheesecloth.
Store the extracted zingiber officinale essential oil in dark glass bottles at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves essential oils helps with difficulty chewing because of its potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, it can numb the pain and reduce swelling, making it easier to chew and swallow. Additionally, cloves oil's warming sensation can help relax tense muscles in the jaw and facial region, further alleviating discomfort and promoting smoother chewing movements. By reducing inflammation and numbing pain, cloves essential oil provides natural relief for individuals experiencing difficulty chewing due to various conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Cloves essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and jaw, making it easier to chew.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial properties of cloves essential oil can help combat oral infections such as gum disease, tooth decay, or abscesses, which may be contributing to difficulty chewing.

Analgesic property: Cloves essential oil has natural analgesic and anesthetic properties that can numb the pain associated with toothaches, mouth sores, or gum recession, making it easier to chew and swallow.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the cloves plant contain a high concentration of eugenol, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with difficulty chewing.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the cloves plant are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the mouth and jaw.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh syzygium aromaticum leaves and stems in the early morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade drying or air circulation at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Crush the dried plant material into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to increase surface area for distillation.
Steam distill the crushed plant material in a still or apparatus with a carrier oil at a ratio of 1:5 for 2-3 hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into dark glass containers for storage and use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram essential oils helps with difficulty chewing because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's natural compounds, such as camphor and thymol, work together to reduce pain and swelling in the jaw and facial muscles, making it easier to chew and swallow. Additionally, the oil's soothing effects can help calm any discomfort or tension associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, allowing for a more comfortable chewing experience. As a result, wild marjoram essential oils can provide relief from the physical discomfort of difficulty chewing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marjoram oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders, making it easier to chew and move your jaw.

Analgesic: Marjoram oil's analgesic properties numb the pain in the mouth and jaw muscles, providing relief from discomfort and difficulty chewing caused by conditions like trigeminal neuralgia or toothaches.
Antispasmodic: Marjoram oil's antispasmodic properties relax muscle spasms in the face, jaw, and tongue, easing tension and cramping that can make it difficult to chew and swallow.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wild marjoram plant contain bioactive compounds like camphor and borneol, which help to reduce inflammation and ease pain in the gums and jaw, making it an effective remedy for difficulty chewing.

Barks: The barks of the wild marjoram plant are rich in tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe irritated tissues and reduce swelling in the mouth and throat, alleviating discomfort while chewing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh origanum vulgare leaves and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the cleaned leaves with 500 milliliters of vodka in a glass jar and store it for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container and discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dry place.
Allow the extract to mature for at least 30 days before using it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with difficulty chewing because its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe sore throats and reduce inflammation in the mouth. The oil's natural expectorant properties can also help to loosen phlegm and mucus, making it easier to chew and swallow. Additionally, thyme oil has a calming effect on the muscles of the jaw and face, reducing tension and discomfort that may be associated with difficulty chewing. Overall, using herbal thyme essential oils can provide relief from discomfort and improve oral function.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Thyme oil's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with difficulty chewing due to conditions like temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorder.

Analgesic properties: Thyme oil's analgesic properties provide relief from pain and discomfort, making it easier to chew and swallow food, especially for individuals suffering from conditions like fibromyalgia or arthritis.

Antimicrobial properties: Thyme oil's antimicrobial properties can help combat infections in the mouth and throat that may be contributing to difficulty chewing, such as gum disease, abscesses, or tonsillitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have been traditionally used to alleviate toothache and gum pain, making them suitable for essential oil blends to help with difficulty chewing.
Leaves: Fresh or dried thyme leaves are often used in herbal teas and infusions to soothe mouth irritations, making them a popular choice for essential oils to ease discomfort when chewing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the early morning after dew has dried completely weighing about 500 grams.
Wash the plant material gently with cold water to remove dirt and debris then pat dry with paper towels.
Steam distill the plant material using a high-quality steam distillation apparatus for 4 hours and 30 minutes collecting the essential oil in a separate container.
Transfer the distilled essential oil into dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for 24 hours allowing sediment to settle before filtering.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cotton cloth removing any remaining impurities and contaminants ensuring clarity and quality.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat difficulty chewing with herbal essential oils is to blend a combination of soothing and anti-inflammatory herbs. Apply a topical mixture of peppermint, eucalyptus, and ginger oils to the affected area using gentle massage strokes. Repeat as needed throughout the day to reduce discomfort and improve gum health. This natural approach can help alleviate symptoms and promote overall oral wellness.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to difficulty chewing that are treated with herbal essential oils are dyspepsia, gastritis, and inflammation of the mouth and throat. Essential oils like peppermint, ginger, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate discomfort and ease swallowing difficulties. Additionally, oils such as fennel and coriander can aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and bloating. These herbal essential oils can be used topically or diffused to promote relaxation and soothe digestive issues.
